title: Vivid Smoky Mexican Corn Tacos
description: Light, yeasty tacos with charred corn, harissa spice, and smoky flavor, perfect for kids and whole30 friends.

ingredients.name: corn tortillas
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought but whole wheat

instructions: Heat a molcajete over medium heat until it begins to smoke lightly.
Add the olive oil and let it swirl around the molcajete for 30 seconds.
Add the harissa spice mix and smoked paprika, grinding them together until fragrant.
Add the garlic and grind it into the spice base for 10 seconds.
Add the fresh corn kernels and grind them lightly to release their juices.
Add the red onion and continue grinding for 20 seconds until the mixture is slightly chunky.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then remove from heat.
In a separate skillet, heat a splash of oil and cook the corn mixture, stirring continuously to create a smoky char.
Transfer the charred corn to a bowl, then add lime juice and chopped cilantro, stirring to combine.
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry skillet or on a griddle, then spoon the smoky corn mixture onto each.
Serve immediately, optionally garnishing with extra cilantro or a squeeze of lime.
